title: tocoyena hispidula
intro: Tocoyena hispidula, also known as the red-flowering tocoyena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tocoyena hispidula include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
